item.title: i. the den of gifts
item.description:
by James Cody Phelan
\

A clamor rang through the morning quiet. Gulls sprung from their roosts above drystone homes and squawked in protest, their days ruined. One squabble of birds drifted over to investigate the commotion and spied a human, only just passing through the dying Koumaria shrubs bundled at the northern edge of the village. The human labored, dragging anger behind him.

item.title: ii. the shape of feeling the experience of forgetting
iii. do aliens dream of football
item.description: You notice the colors first. Tangerine orange against lapis; vermillion against daisy yellow. The shades enwrap each man and his carapace of beetle-like armor as he enters the field of green. Supervisory figures wears stripes of zebra hide, their greyscale in obvious distinction to abundant polychrome. At either edge of the field, beyond the painted hectares, stand neon-yellow metal tongs bifurcated to the sky, as the sky rests bluer than the day it was born. The colors twist and fold, over and behind as men jockey across the constructed terrain. There they smash, sprinting, into their opposite partner, their colors overlaid in a competition of visual noise you cannot ignore. It is fabulous.

item.title: iv. title
item.description: TESTING TESTING TESTING. Is this thing on? Blogito ergo sume

item.title: the project
item.description:
Ten pieces of writing on a theme.

irreconcilable
adjective
/ˌɪr.ek.ənˈsaɪ.lə.bəl/
impossible to make consistent or congruous

irreconcilable is a project about the gaps between the way things are and the way we want them to be: the unresolved desires that stick to us as nagging discomforts.
